Use future forms such as: ‘I’m going to go to a music festival’, ‘I’m taking a tent, T-shirt and shorts…’, ‘I’m going to listen to pop and dance music’ and ‘Maybe it’ll rain’.

I’ve found an absolutely amazing job for you. Let me tell you why you should apply...

Look at your rubric. Practice writing a sentence for each guideline. Now write the rejection letter, following the rubric.

writtenTask: You have a friend who is experiencing a lot of stress from his job. He lives alone and has no immediate social support. Write a letter that expresses your understanding of his situation and gives some advice on managing stress.

title: Jokes and humor
writtenTask: Task 1: You're stuck in an elevator! Help is on the way, but you and the other people who are stuck are trying to tell stories to ease the tension. Tell a funny story to the other people. Task 2: Your story was a success! In fact, one of the passengers has asked you to write it down and email it to him. Write your funny story. Useful phrases: You’ll never believe this, but… The most incredible thing happened… It was so alarming… Everything happened really fast. ...really lucked out.

title: In the news
writtenTask: Task 1: You're a journalist covering a big murder trial. Your connection at the police station has given you the interview tapes. Listen and make notes about what happened. Task 2: It's time to write your report. Write the story, adding some details of your own to 'spice it up'. Use the audio to help you.

title: Communication
topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
writtenTask: A group of international buyers are visiting your company next month. They have asked you for some advice about business etiquette in your country. Write an email to the head of the delegation and tell him about the local customs, gestures, body language and rules of etiquette where you're from.

writtenTask: Imagine you are a family counselor writing a magazine article on NLP reframing. Listen to one of your clients talk about how she has successfully applied NLP reframing to a situation in her life. Then summarize her experience to use as an example in the magazine article. Write about Before NLP, After NLP, Techniques Applied.

title: The entertainment industry
writtenTask: Your work inspires you to make your own movie. Remember to include the title, the setting, and the characters. You can also choose actors to play each of your main characters. You should make a list of important crew members you will need and what they will be required to do.

title: E-commerce
writtenTask: Task 1: You're going to write a comparison review of two online retail websites. Read the notes then rank the issues in the order you think most important. Task 2: Now write a comparative review of the two websites. Structure your review around the aspects of an online retail website you think most important. Pay attention to the comparative structures you use in your text.

title: Urban issues
topic: Writing an article about a superstore
writtenTask: Task 1: You work as a local reporter. The new big-box store Super-Mart is planning to open in your town. Many citizens are concerned about this and have gathered for a meeting. Read what their concerns are and make notes. Task 2: Now write a letter to the mayor to express your point of view about Super-Mart. Start your letter like this: Dear Mayor Bleak, I am writing to you to...

title: Quality of life
writtenTask: As a result of your research and interest in the topic, you’ve been commissioned to write an article for a lifestyles magazine about how people’s lives will be different in 20 years from now. This article should be based on your opinions. You could consider work situations, home life, transport and the environment.

title: Meaning and symbols
writtenTask: Look at the three pictures and write your interpretation of their content or meaning. Each picture represents a prophecy or prediction related to the future of mankind. Describe the events portrayed in each picture.

title: Science and technology
writtenTask: Write a short report comparing the three robots for the web-based magazine.

title: National identity
topic: Writing about a symbol of your country
writtenTask: You've decided to enter an essay writing competition. Read the details, choose and research a symbol from where you live or one that you're interested in, then write your essay.

title: Tough choices
writtenTask: Task 1: You are a trainee journalist who decides to read about professional ethics before choosing which story to write. Here are some notes that you have made about potential stories. Task 2: Read them again in the light of what you have learnt about ethics and choose which story is the only one that you can write ethically, that is to say in accordance with the code of ethics from Activity 1. Task 3: Here are some further notes that you took at the press conference about the arrest. Read them and then write your article. Use an appropriate structure and journalistic style of language.

title: Fame and fortune
writtenTask: You and your friend are at the Star Attack seminar. For your final assignment, you have to choose one of the three celebrities from the website and ‘throw a brick’ at them. Write to the website, explaining which celebrity you want to attack, and why.

title: Financial planning
writtenTask: You want to buy a house and have decided to apply for a bank loan. Look at the documents (your driver’s license, credit card, bank statement and the property purchase contract) and note down information you think you will need to fill out an application for a bank loan. Write between 150-180 words. Type your answer into the input box.

title: Dealing with stress
writtenTask: A visualization script is a description of an environment that you find peaceful and calming, and that helps you to relax. Your therapist wants you to use the triggers that help you relax to write a visualization script to help you drift off to sleep. Read the example and then write a visualization script of your own.

title: Doing research
writtenTask: Task 1: You are studying to be a cryptozoologist. Cryptozoologists research creatures that are considered legendary or non-existent. Look at the list and decide which you would most like to research. You can choose more than one. Task 2: Choose one of the legendary creatures on your list and research it on the Internet. Take notes. Task 3: Take the information you gathered from your research and write a summary to hand in for your assignment.
